article.headline: Krunal, Kohli and Salt thrash KKR on opening night of IPL 2025
article.description: KKR lost their way after Rahane and Narine's 103-run partnership for the second wicket

images.name: cricket:image:1477712 [1400x933]
images.caption: Phil Salt and Virat Kohli added 95 for the opening wicket

images.name: Watch - Kohli smashes Varun over long-on
images.caption: He slogs the mysteryspinner for half a dozen

comments.text: Johnson to Livingstone, FOUR, Whacked to the right of mid-on and 17 years after RCB were thumped by KKR in an IPL opener, they return the favour. It finishes with a length ball outside off that was heaved across with a semi-horizontal bat.
comments.post: RCB's NRR is above two after just one game. The defending champions will travel to Guwahati to face RR, RCB will travel to Chennai to play CSK. Do check out the report, analysis from this game. Hope to see you for the double header tomorrow. For now, it's goodbye from me Ekanth, Ashish Pant who was also on comms, and our scorers, Ranjith P and Venkat Raghav. Krunal Pandya, POTM: I just wanted to narrow down my focus upon returning in the 11th over. Glad I was able to deliver. You have to go with the flow, right now batters can hit sixes consistently, so you have to find ways to up your game. So, I wanted to bowl quick, changes of pace were useful too. Jitesh knows I can do anything, bowl a wide yorker or bouncer, so he's aware. You have to use what you have in your arsenal. Once I got into the team, I got to know about the madness. Even when I was playing domestic cricket in Bangalore, there were RCB chants. It's been a great feeling in the last ten days with the team. Rajat Patidar, RCB captain: There was pressure, of course, but it was a good day for me. Hope for more such days. (Bringing back Suyash for the fourth over) It was clear we wanted Russell's wicket, him conceding runs didn't matter. He is our strike bowler, we backed him. All credit goes to Krunal and Suyash, they were 130 off, the bowlers showed courage and determination. Feels great to captain Kohli, he supports a lot, great opportunity to learn from one of the best. (On the shot on 14.6 - a lifted square drive off a length ball) It was a pre-determined shot, I wanted to go over cover. Phil Salt: Kohli and I haven't batted together much, to put on a partnership like that was key. It's a ground I know, had a simple game plan coming in. Wanted to go on longer, but not to be. Batting second here makes a bit of difference, you have to hit through the line. Ajinkya Rahane, KKR captain: We went well till the 13th over, 2-3 wickets changed momentum. We have to learn from our mistakes,. We thought 210-220 was par when Venky and I were batting but we lost wickets. There was dew but they had a very good powerplay. We couldn't get early wickets. We don't want to think too much about this game, just want to improve as a unit. Saty: "Supporting RCB is an emotional rollercoaster--one day it's 'we will never improve,' the next it's 'this is our year!' The struggle is real!" 10:47pm: A comprehensive win for RCB. They were behind 10 overs into the game, but Krunal Pandya helped them stage a comeback with the ball. With the bat, Phil Salt blew the hosts out of the water with powerplay power-hitting. Kohli's sixes off Johnson in the fifth over were great too. Ten fours and four sixes, 80 for 0 in six overs, that was way too big an advantage on a pitch that was batter-friendly. There was a slight slowdown with the first two wickets but Patidar batted like an artist, caressing and whacking the ball with sheer effortlessness. Kohli stayed on till the end, and the visitors completed the win with 22 balls to spare.

comments.text: Johnson to Livingstone, SIX, Hooked over RCB's dugout. Livingstone greeted with another short ball, this wasn't bad. Around top of shoulder height, fourth-stump line. The batter went across, got inside the line of the ball, leant to the off side and swung in front of his face. Went very fine, and well over deep fine leg
comments.pre: Final over of the game? Johnson is back

comments.text: Arora to Livingstone, 1 run, Length just outside off, square driven to deep point
comments.post: Partha Chatterj: "KKR have looked really rusty tonight except for the first 10 overs, no plan and no desperation evident from the players, even the fielding was average"

comments.text: Arora to Livingstone, no run, Under-edged to the keeper on the bounce. Was a length ball on fifth that Livingstone tried to slap to the leg side with a horizontal bat
comments.post: Replays showing Kohli clutching his back after completing a run earlier. He's okay to continue as of now though

comments.text: Arora to Livingstone, FOUR, Pulled just behind square. Half-tracker at chest-height, Livingstone's head turned leg side as the bat swooshed across, but it was in one full motion
comments.pre: Prasanna : "RCB should send Jitesh next, their designated finisher.." It'll be Livingstone, who's also a finisher

comments.text: Arora to Patidar, OUT, Pulled, timing's not bad but he finds deep midwicket. Great innings nonetheless, just 13 now needed off 27. Was a length ball around off, another languid bat swing, Rinku ran in and took it on the move
comments.dismissalText: RM Patidar c Singh b Arora 34 (17m 16b 5x4 1x6) SR: 212.5
comments.post:
RaoP: "wish the  IPL had the concept of bonus points.  would make one sided chases like this even more interesting. "

Tcj: "14.6 - That's the shot of the match right there."

Timeout

overs.endOfOverRunsReq: (8 runs required from 24 balls, RR: 10.43, RRR: 2.00)

partCommentary.commentPreText: Final over of the game? Johnson is back

batsmen.name: Q de Kock
batsmen.commentary: De Kock can't cash in on the reprieve. He is out of here two balls later. Sharp back of a length ball shaping into de Kock. He is on the move looking to punch this through covers. Is too close to the ball, and can only manage a thick inside-edge to the right of the keeper who takes a lovely catch low.

batsmen.name: SP Narine
batsmen.commentary: Finally, finally a wicket for Rasikh and he lets Narine know! Sharp back of a length ball at 132kph outside off. He throws his bat at it, gets a thick outside edge, and Jitesh does the rest.

batsmen.name: AM Rahane
batsmen.commentary: Pulled straight to deep-backward square leg, that's Rahane falling into the trap. Looked like he wanted to clear the boundary but couldn't generate enough power on the 98ks length ball on the pads. Shot was on, he went deep in the crease, and tried to lift it from down to up while on one knee. Just ended up finding the man. Two quick wickets for RCB

batsmen.name: VR Iyer
batsmen.commentary: RCB continue to fight back, Krunal's pace outdoes Venkatesh. 101ks, on a length, angling away from around the wicket through fourth stump. Venkatesh was upright for a while, then one foot went up as he tried to force a flick through midwicket. Inside edged it onto middle. Krunal pumps his fists with ferocity

batsmen.name: A Raghuvanshi
batsmen.commentary: Sound as ball passes bat, yes, there's a feather as Raghuvanshi pulled. 134ks scramble-seam ball on a shortish length lands around off and angles away. The batter tried to whack it square from the top of the bounce.

batsmen.name: RK Singh
batsmen.commentary: Stump-to-stump does it again. Krunal completes his spell by knocking top of off, he's ensured KKR couldn't run away with the game in the middle overs after a flier. No turn on that, didn't look that short. Rinku went for the pull but that skidded through, went below the bat

batsmen.name: AD Russell
batsmen.commentary: Suyash gets Russell with a googly, just 87ks. Landed on fourth, may have drifted away a touch. Spun back sharply, Russell committed to the shot and got nothing on it. Middle stump and leg stump form a V. 107 for 1 has turned into 150 for 6.

batsmen.name: Harshit Rana
batsmen.commentary: Another pull, this is sliced though. Skied and the keeper runs to the left, gets in line with the ball and takes it while reaching forward.

batsmen.name: PD Salt
batsmen.commentary: Thick outside edge and Johnson hangs on at short third. A much needed breakthrough for KKR. Legspinner well outside off. Salt throws his bat at it, gets the thick edge, and Johnson moves well to his left

batsmen.name: D Padikkal
batsmen.commentary: Padikkal perishes trying to take Narine on, holes out to deep midwicket but Kohli gives him a fist bump on the way out. He danced down the wicket and tried to whip the length ball on the up, bat-face turned inside upon impact. Went straight to the fielder.

batsmen.name: RM Patidar
batsmen.commentary: Pulled, timing's not bad but he finds deep midwicket. Great innings nonetheless, just 13 now needed off 27. Was a length ball around off, another languid bat swing, Rinku ran in and took it on the move

innings.lastBat: Rajat Patidar c Singh b Arora 34 (17m 16b 5X4 1X6) SR: 212.5
innings.partnership: 15 runs, 0.5 overs, RR: 18 (Virat Kohli 0, Liam Livingstone 15)

wickets.text:
0.5 Hazlewood to de Kock, OUT
De Kock can't cash in on the reprieve. He is out of here two balls later. Sharp back of a length ball shaping into de Kock. He is on the move looking to punch this through covers. Is too close to the ball, and can only manage a thick inside-edge to the right of the keeper who takes a lovely catch low.
c †Sharma b Hazlewood 4 (3m 5b 1x4 0x6)
SR: 80
Fall of wicket: 4/1

9.6 Rasikh Salam to Narine, OUT
Finally, finally a wicket for Rasikh and he lets Narine know! Sharp back of a length ball at 132kph outside off. He throws his bat at it, gets a thick outside edge, and Jitesh does the rest.
c †Sharma b Rasikh Salam 44 (52m 26b 5x4 3x6)
SR: 169.23
Fall of wicket: 107/2

10.3 Pandya to Rahane, OUT
Pulled straight to deep-backward square leg, that's Rahane falling into the trap. Looked like he wanted to clear the boundary but couldn't generate enough power on the 98ks length ball on the pads. Shot was on, he went deep in the crease, and tried to lift it from down to up while on one knee. Just ended up finding the man. Two quick wickets for RCB
c Rasikh Salam b Pandya 56 (51m 31b 6x4 4x6)
SR: 180.64
Fall of wicket: 109/3

12.1 Pandya to Iyer, OUT
RCB continue to fight back, Krunal's pace outdoes Venkatesh. 101ks, on a length, angling away from around the wicket through fourth stump. Venkatesh was upright for a while, then one foot went up as he tried to force a flick through midwicket. Inside edged it onto middle. Krunal pumps his fists with ferocity
b Pandya 6 (11m 7b 1x4 0x6)
SR: 85.71
Fall of wicket: 125/4

14.6 Pandya to Singh, OUT
Stump-to-stump does it again. Krunal completes his spell by knocking top of off, he's ensured KKR couldn't run away with the game in the middle overs after a flier. No turn on that, didn't look that short. Rinku went for the pull but that skidded through, went below the bat
b Pandya 12 (10m 10b 1x4 0x6)
SR: 120
Fall of wicket: 145/5

15.4 Suyash Sharma to Russell, OUT
Suyash gets Russell with a googly, just 87ks. Landed on fourth, may have drifted away a touch. Spun back sharply, Russell committed to the shot and got nothing on it. Middle stump and leg stump form a V. 107 for 1 has turned into 150 for 6.
b Suyash Sharma 4 (2m 3b 1x4 0x6)
SR: 133.33
Fall of wicket: 150/6

18.5 Yash Dayal to Raghuvanshi, OUT
Sound as ball passes bat, yes, there's a feather as Raghuvanshi pulled. 134ks scramble-seam ball on a shortish length lands around off and angles away. The batter tried to whack it square from the top of the bounce.
c †Sharma b Yash Dayal 30 (36m 22b 2x4 1x6)
SR: 136.36
Fall of wicket: 168/7

19.5 Hazlewood to Harshit Rana, OUT
Another pull, this is sliced though. Skied and the keeper runs to the left, gets in line with the ball and takes it while reaching forward.
c †Sharma b Hazlewood 5 (4m 6b 1x4 0x6)
SR: 83.33
Fall of wicket: 173/8

8.3 Varun to Salt, OUT
Thick outside edge and Johnson hangs on at short third. A much needed breakthrough for KKR. Legspinner well outside off. Salt throws his bat at it, gets the thick edge, and Johnson moves well to his left
c Johnson b Varun 56 (40m 31b 9x4 2x6)
SR: 180.64
Fall of wicket: 95/1

11.4 Narine to Padikkal, OUT
Padikkal perishes trying to take Narine on, holes out to deep midwicket but Kohli gives him a fist bump on the way out. He danced down the wicket and tried to whip the length ball on the up, bat-face turned inside upon impact. Went straight to the fielder.
c Ramandeep Singh b Narine 10 (11m 10b 1x4 0x6)
SR: 100
Fall of wicket: 118/2

15.3 Arora to Patidar, OUT
Pulled, timing's not bad but he finds deep midwicket. Great innings nonetheless, just 13 now needed off 27. Was a length ball around off, another languid bat swing, Rinku ran in and took it on the move
c Singh b Arora 34 (17m 16b 5x4 1x6)
SR: 212.5
Fall of wicket: 162/3
